<img src="https://r2cdn.perplexity.ai/pplx-full-logo-primary-dark%402x.png" style="height:64px;margin-right:32px"/>

# Python in Excel code example for competitive pricing analysis

Python in Excel uses the =PY() function to embed Pandas, NumPy, and scikit-learn code directly in cells, perfect for competitive pricing analysis on your telecom deals. Paste these snippets into Excel (Microsoft 365 required) for instant ML-driven insights from costs, competitor data, and volumes.[^1][^2]

## Core Code Example: Elasticity \& Optimal Price Prediction

In an Excel cell, enter =PY( followed by this code (adapt your data range A1:F6 for inputs):

```
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression

# Your data (replace with Excel range xl("A1:F6", headers=True))
data = {
    'cost': [100, 150, 200, 120, 180],
    'competitor_price': [130, 170, 220, 140, 190],
    'proposed_price': [145, 165, 235, 155, 195],
    'demand_volume': [1000, 800, 600, 950, 700],
    'complexity': [2, 4, 3, 1, 5]
}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Price gap and elasticity proxy
df['price_gap'] = df['proposed_price'] - df['competitor_price']
df['elasticity'] = np.log(df['demand_volume']) / np.log(df['proposed_price'] / df['cost'])

# ML model: Predict optimal price
X = df[['cost', 'competitor_price', 'complexity']]
y = df['proposed_price']
model = LinearRegression().fit(X, y)
df['optimal_price'] = model.predict(X)
df[['price_gap', 'elasticity', 'optimal_price']].round(2)
```

This outputs a table with gaps, elasticity, and ML-suggested prices—e.g., for a new deal (cost=160, comp=175, complexity=3), predicts ~185 ensuring competitive edge.

## Demo Results

Summary from sample run:


| deal_id | price_gap | elasticity_proxy | predicted_margin | optimal_price_ml |
| :-- | :-- | :-- | :-- | :-- |
| Deal1 | 15 | 18.591 | 0.402 | 140.21 |
| Deal2 | -5 | 70.135 | 0.157 | 173.51 |
| Deal3 | 15 | 39.666 | 0.171 | 234.20 |
| Deal4 | 15 | 26.790 | 0.307 | 156.86 |
| Deal5 | 5 | 81.845 | 0.057 | 190.21 |

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Model emphasizes competitor_price (coef ~1.09), ideal for quick deal tweaks in Excel without Replit export.[^3]
